title: Risk + Sector Promotion Program
objective: Test whether the broad momentum branch can recover promotion-grade walk-forward robustness by keeping the gross-exposure risk seed and then adding a sector-cap follow-up without giving back the holdout improvement.
branch_rationale: This branch replaced the older broad momentum and regime-overlay paths because queued risk and sector work produced the best repo-backed holdout direction seen so far, even though promotion policy was still failing.
positive_hypotheses: The gross65_deploy20_n8_w09_cb12 risk seed materially improved holdout behaviour versus the earlier broad beta control and is the right control for this family.
The sec3 follow-up is the strongest recorded sector path for this family and is the last defined attempt to recover validation and walk-forward evidence without abandoning the holdout gain.

seed_stack.label: Broad control
seed_stack.rationale: Reference broad candidate family before the risk branch.

seed_stack.label: Risk seed
seed_stack.rationale: Best holdout-improving risk branch and current family control.

seed_stack.label: Sector follow-up
seed_stack.rationale: Strongest combined split result on the risk-seed family and the final defined follow-up before retirement.

campaign_path.label: Risk Seed
campaign_path.purpose: Lock the best risk-controlled seed and use it as the branch control.

campaign_path.label: Sector Follow-Up
campaign_path.purpose: Restore validation and walk-forward strength while preserving the improved holdout profile from the risk seed.

stop_conditions.when: Any step in the branch passes the existing promotion policy and remains acceptable for downstream paper-trading preparation.
The final sector follow-up has a recorded promotion decision and still fails the policy, especially on walk-forward, validation, or holdout thresholds.
